predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, the Lloyd’s Market Association Joint War Committee (JWC) will issue a public listed-areas update or circular that newly adds OR materially expands a listed war-risk area covering the Strait of Hormuz and/or Gulf of Oman.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if an LMA/JWC published listed-areas document/circular (on LMA site or widely republished by major brokers/insurers) explicitly names the Strait of Hormuz and/or Gulf of Oman as newly listed/expanded compared with the prior version.
monitoring_signals: LMA/JWC listed areas updates
War-risk broker notes from Marsh, Howden, Aon
P&I club advisories for Middle East transits
Reuters/TradeWinds reporting on war-risk classifications
predictions.rationale: Escalation risk around Hormuz often triggers insurance-market geographic reclassification; a formal JWC update is a common mechanism.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, a major shipping broker/index provider (Clarksons, Baltic Exchange, or Platts) will report Middle East–linked VLCC spot earnings at or above $150,000/day on at least one day (or one published daily assessment).
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if a published daily spot-earnings assessment or index print from Clarksons/Baltic/Platts shows VLCC spot earnings >= $150,000/day for a Middle East load route (or explicitly described as Middle East-linked VLCC earnings).
monitoring_signals: Baltic Dirty Tanker Index components
Clarksons Research daily tanker earnings
Platts tanker marketwire headlines
Middle East loadings disruption headlines
predictions.rationale: Heightened war-risk and rerouting typically tightens tanker supply and can spike VLCC earnings quickly.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, a major marine insurance broker (Marsh, Howden, Aon, Gallagher, or Willis) will publish a client note stating that quoted war-risk additional premium for a single Strait of Hormuz transit reached at least 0.50% of hull value (or equivalent wording).
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if any publicly posted broker market update/client note (or Reuters quoting the note) explicitly states Hormuz transit war-risk additional premium (AP) at >=0.50% of hull value (or a clearly equivalent percentage quote).
monitoring_signals: Broker war-risk bulletins (Marsh/Howden/Aon)
Reuters insurance-market reports on Gulf war-risk pricing
P&I club circulars referencing AP levels
Shipowner comments on insurance costs
predictions.rationale: If escalation persists, war-risk AP often reprices sharply; brokers frequently quantify the move in published market notes.

predictions.text: By 2026-09-30, the Federal Reserve will raise the target range for the federal funds rate by at least 25 basis points versus the target range in effect on 2026-03-29.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if an FOMC statement (federalreserve.gov) shows the target range upper bound at least 0.25 percentage points higher than the range effective on 2026-03-29.
monitoring_signals: Fed funds futures (CME) implied probabilities
Core CPI/PCE prints and inflation expectations
Energy price persistence and pass-through indicators
FOMC minutes and speeches referencing oil-driven inflation
predictions.rationale: Market-implied hike odds rise when energy shocks threaten inflation expectations; follow-through is plausible within two to three meetings.

predictions.text: By 2026-08-31, at least one major U.S. passenger airline (Delta, United, American, Southwest, JetBlue, or Alaska) will publicly implement a temporary 'fuel surcharge' or 'energy surcharge' applicable to passenger tickets (not only cargo), disclosed in a press release, investor update, or fare-rule filing.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if an airline public communication or fare-rule/filing (or Reuters/WSJ citing such documents) shows a passenger-ticket surcharge explicitly labeled fuel/energy surcharge, with an effective date on or before 2026-08-31.
monitoring_signals: Airline fare-rule updates (ATPCO references in reporting)
Earnings call transcripts discussing surcharges
DOT complaints/industry reporting on fare add-ons
Jet fuel crack spreads and airline margin commentary
predictions.rationale: If fuel spikes persist, airlines sometimes shift from hedging/fees to explicit passenger surcharges, especially for shorter-haul markets.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, the Bank of Russia will raise its key rate by at least 100 basis points in a single scheduled policy decision (one meeting-to-meeting move).
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if the Bank of Russia press release on a scheduled policy meeting states the key rate increased by >=1.00 percentage point versus the prior meeting level.
monitoring_signals: Russian CPI and inflation expectations prints
Ruble volatility and capital controls changes
Fiscal measures increasing extraction/taxes
Bank of Russia meeting previews and guidance language
predictions.rationale: Fiscal extraction and sanctions-era constraints can worsen inflation dynamics, increasing the likelihood of a large defensive rate move.

predictions.text: By 2026-09-30, South Korea will publish an official implementation measure for the public-parking-lot solar mandate (e.g., enforcement decree, ministry guideline, or procurement standard) that specifies compliance timing and covered parking-lot categories.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if a Korean government gazette posting or ministry website (MOTIE, MOLIT, MOE, or equivalent) publishes a rule/guideline for the mandate that includes at least (a) covered entities/categories and (b) a compliance deadline or phased schedule.
monitoring_signals: Korean Official Gazette regulatory postings
MOTIE/MOLIT press releases and implementation Q&A
Public procurement bid notices for solar canopies
Utility interconnection rule updates for parking-lot PV
predictions.rationale: A mandate typically requires implementing rules within months to define scope, enforcement, and financing/procurement mechanics.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, Meta will publicly announce production deployment (not just R&D) of an Arm-based custom general-purpose data-center CPU (distinct from AI accelerators), naming the chip/program and stating it is running in Meta data centers.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if Meta publishes a newsroom post, engineering blog, or earnings materials explicitly stating an Arm-based custom CPU is deployed/operational in Meta data centers and provides a chip/program name (or clear identifier).
monitoring_signals: Meta engineering blog posts about infrastructure silicon
Earnings calls mentioning CPU offload/cost-per-compute gains
Arm partner announcements and ecosystem presentations
Open Compute Project (OCP) disclosures from Meta
predictions.rationale: The partnership signals intent to verticalize silicon; a named, deployed CPU is a natural next milestone for cost/power optimization.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the European Commission will open a formal Digital Services Act (DSA) proceeding (or equivalent formal investigation announcement) against an AI chatbot/companion service, explicitly citing risks to minors and/or mental-health harms from conversational AI outputs.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verified if the European Commission publishes a press release or official webpage stating it has opened a formal DSA proceeding/investigation into a named AI chatbot/companion service, and the announcement explicitly references minors and/or mental-health/self-harm risk.
monitoring_signals: EU Commission DSA enforcement announcements
NGO/academic reports on chatbot harms triggering regulator attention
Member-state referrals to the Commission
Platform risk-assessment and transparency report disclosures
predictions.rationale: Growing evidence of harmful advice dynamics increases regulatory pressure; the EU has established tools to open formal platform-risk proceedings.
